    40	// QueueEntry is one row in the PreviewQueue result. Position is 1-based;
    41	// FilterDecision and Why explain why the picker would or would not pick this
    42	// bead as the next claim.
    43	type QueueEntry struct {
    44		Position       int            `json:"position"`
    45		BeadID         string         `json:"id"`
    46		Title          string         `json:"title"`
    47		Priority       int            `json:"priority"`
    48		QueueRank      *int           `json:"queue_rank,omitempty"`
    49		UpdatedAt      time.Time      `json:"updated_at"`
    50		Status         string         `json:"status"`
    51		FilterDecision FilterDecision `json:"filter_decision"`
    52		Why            string         `json:"why"`
    53	}
    54	
    55	// PreviewQueue returns the bead execution queue in picker order, annotated with
    56	// filter decisions and skip reasons for each entry. It applies the same filter
    57	// logic as ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate (label_filter intersection) but
    58	// intentionally omits the per-Run attempted map (non-deterministic across runs).
    59	//
    60	// The returned slice is ordered identically to what ReadyExecution returns —
    61	// priority asc, then queue-rank asc, then created_at asc, then id within a
    62	// priority bucket. The first entry whose FilterDecision is FilterDecisionNext
    63	// is the bead nextCandidate would return on a fresh Run (before any claim).
    64	//
    65	// Limit controls the maximum number of entries returned. 0 means no limit
    66	// (return all entries from ReadyExecution).
    67	func PreviewQueue(store PreviewQueueStore, filters PickerFilters, limit int) ([]QueueEntry, error) {
    68		ready, err := store.ReadyExecution()
    69		if err != nil {
    70			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preview queue: %w", err)
    71		}
    72	
    73		foundFirst := false
    74		var entries []QueueEntry
    75		for _, b := range ready {
    76			entry := QueueEntry{
    77				Position:  len(entries) + 1,
    78				BeadID:    b.ID,
    79				Title:     b.Title,
    80				Priority:  b.Priority,
    81				QueueRank: queueRankPtr(b.Extra["queue-rank"]),
    82				UpdatedAt: b.UpdatedAt,
    83				Status:    b.Status,
    84			}
    85	
    86			// Apply label filter — same logic as nextCandidate.
    87			if filters.LabelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(b.Labels, filters.LabelFilter) {
    88				entry.FilterDecision = FilterDecisionSkipped
    89				entry.Why = fmt.Sprintf("skipped: label_filter mismatch (bead labels=%v; worker filter=[%s])", b.Labels, filters.LabelFilter)
    90				entries = append(entries, entry)
    91				if limit > 0 && len(entries) >= limit {
    92					break
    93				}
    94				continue
    95			}
    96	
    97			// Capabilities filter — no-op today; reserved for future matching.
    98			// When implemented, add a skip reason here mirroring label_filter.
    99	
   100			// This bead is eligible. The first eligible bead is the "next claim".
   101			if !foundFirst {
   102				foundFirst = true
   103				entry.FilterDecision = FilterDecisionNext
   104				entry.Why = "next claim"
   105			} else {
   106				entry.FilterDecision = FilterDecisionEligible
   107				entry.Why = fmt.Sprintf("eligible (rank %d)", entry.Position)
   108			}
   109			entries = append(entries, entry)
   110			if limit > 0 && len(entries) >= limit {
   111				break
   112			}
   113		}
   114	
   115		return entries, nil
   116	}

    30	Unlike 'ddx bead ready', plan applies the picker's filters:
    31	  - Execution eligibility (cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible flag)
    32	  - Optional label-filter intersection (--label-filter)
    33	  - Optional capabilities constraint (--capabilities; currently a no-op)

    35	The per-Run attempted map is intentionally excluded: it is non-deterministic

    38	Columns:

    42	  RANK     — queue-rank within the priority bucket, when present
    43	  UPDATED  — last updated timestamp (RFC3339)
    44	  STATUS   — bead status

   205	// honors queue-rank within a priority bucket and still falls back to the next
   206	// ranked bead when the lowest-ranked candidate is already attempted.
   207	func TestPicker_QueueRankOrdering(t *testing.T) {
   208		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())

   211		// Create two same-priority beads with different queue-rank values.

   216	
   217		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: store}
   218

   228		got2, skips2, ok2, err := worker.nextCandidate(context.Background(), results, nil, "", "")
   229		require.NoError(t, err)

   231		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-rank-9", got2.ID)

   210	// passes over a higher-priority bead (because it is in the per-Run
   211	// `attempted` map from a prior iteration) and claims a lower-priority bead,
   212	// the loop emits a structured picker.priority_skip event naming the skipped
   213	// bead, the reason, and the queue-rank when present. Without this, future
   214	// starvation regressions would be invisible to operators.
   215	func TestPickerPrioritySkip_IncludesQueueRank(t *testing.T) {
   216		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
   217		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
   218	
   219		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
   220			ID:       "ddx-p0-skipped",
   221			Title:    "P0 will fail preflight",
   222			Priority: 0,
   223			Extra:    map[string]any{"queue-rank": 7},
   224		}))
   225		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-p2-claimed", Title: "P2 fallback", Priority: 2}))
   226	
   227		// Preflight returns nil for the P2 but a non-nil error for the P0.
   228		// The current loop responds to preflight rejection by returning early —
   229		// not what we want here, since we need to demonstrate priority_skip
   230		// after a higher-priority bead is parked in `attempted`. Instead, use
   231		// the pre-claim hook: it conditionally fails on the P0 and on the
   232		// second presentation of the P0 the loop adds it to attempted, so the
   233		// next candidate becomes the P2.
   234		hookCalls := 0
   235		preHook := func(ctx context.Context) error {
   236			hookCalls++
   237			// Always pass — we want to demonstrate the skip via a different
   238			// mechanism: use ComplexityGate instead. Set this aside; see below.
   239			return nil
   240		}
   241		_ = preHook
   242	
   243		// Use ComplexityGate to mark the P0 as "do not claim" — the loop adds
   244		// the bead to attempted after the gate signals shouldClaim=false (via
   245		// the implicit attempted-add prior to Claim is bypassed in the
   246		// not-shouldClaim path; instead the gate's own bookkeeping is its
   247		// concern). Here we simulate by having the gate return shouldClaim=true
   248		// for both, but with the P0 "already attempted" by seeding the loop a
   249		// different way.
   250		//
   251		// Simplest: drive two iterations. First iteration: both ready. Loop
   252		// claims P0, executor returns no_changes which (per loop semantics)
   253		// loops back. Then on second iteration, P0 is now in `attempted`
   254		// (added pre-Claim), so picker selects P2. The P2 claim should emit
   255		// picker.priority_skip naming the P0 with reason=in_attempted.
   256		var calls atomic.Int32
   257		executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
   258			calls.Add(1)
   259			if beadID == "ddx-p0-skipped" {
   260				// no_changes leaves bead open. The loop adds it to attempted
   261				// (line ~480) BEFORE Claim, so on the next iteration the
   262				// picker will skip it with reason=in_attempted.
   263				return ExecuteBeadReport{
   264					BeadID: beadID,
   265					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
   266					Detail: "nothing to do",
   267				}, nil
   268			}
   269			return ExecuteBeadReport{
   270				BeadID:    beadID,
   271				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
   272				SessionID: "sess",
   273				ResultRev: "rev",
   274			}, nil
   275		})
   276	
   277		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: store, Executor: executor}
   278	
   279		var sink eventCaptureWriter
   280		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker-skip"}
   281		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
   282		// Drain the queue: both beads handled in one Run (Once=false, but we
   283		// stop when no candidates remain since PollInterval=0).
   284		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
   285			EventSink:    &sink,
   286			SessionID:    "sess-test",
   287			PollInterval: 0,
   288		})
   289		require.NoError(t, err)
   290	
   291		// Expect at least one picker.priority_skip event with chosen=ddx-p2-claimed
   292		// and skipped containing ddx-p0-skipped with reason=in_attempted.
   293		found := false
   294		for _, evt := range sink.events() {
   295			if evt["type"] != "picker.priority_skip" {
   296				continue
   297			}
   298			data, ok := evt["data"].(map[string]any)
   299			if !ok {
   300				continue
   301			}
   302			if data["chosen_bead_id"] != "ddx-p2-claimed" {
   303				continue
   304			}
   305			skipped, ok := data["skipped"].([]any)
   306			if !ok || len(skipped) == 0 {
   307				continue
   308			}
   309			first, _ := skipped[0].(map[string]any)
   310			if first["bead_id"] == "ddx-p0-skipped" && first["reason"] == "in_attempted" && first["queue_rank"] == float64(7) {
   311				found = true
   312				break
   313			}
   314		}
   315		require.True(t, found,
   316			"expected picker.priority_skip event for chosen=ddx-p2-claimed skipping ddx-p0-skipped (reason=in_attempted, queue_rank=7); got events:\n%s",
   317			sink.dump())
   318	}

   321	// can assert on the structured stream without parsing the human log.
   322	type eventCaptureWriter struct {

   326	
   327	func (w *eventCaptureWriter) Write(p []byte) (int, error) {
   328		w.mu.Lock()
   329		defer w.mu.Unlock()
   330		w.buf = append(w.buf, p...)

Implemented queue-rank visibility end-to-end.

- `PreviewQueue` now carries `queue_rank` on each entry when present, omitting it when absent, and its ordering comment now matches `priority -> queue-rank -> created_at -> id` in [cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a089143b-20260506T160143-7c1f3a0f/cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go#L43) and [cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a089143b-20260506T160143-7c1f3a0f/cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go#L60).
